US Defense Orders Surge to Near-Record Amid War With Iran
items.summary: US orders for defense capital goods surged in April to the second-highest level on record, adding to signs that the Iran war is boosting demand for military hardware.

Petrobras Hikes Gasoline Prices After Brazil Subsidies Approved
items.summary: Brazil’s oil producer Petrobras is increasing domestic gasoline prices for distributors after the government approved new subsidies to shield consumers from Iran war-fueled crude price hikes.

items.title: US and Iran Reach Deal to Extend Truce, Axios Reports
items.summary: Iran and the US have reached a deal to extend a truce and work toward an agreement to end the war, Axios reported, sending oil prices lower. Tyler Kendall reports on "Bloomberg Open Interest." (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Treasuries Rise After Reported US-Iran Deal to Extend Truce
items.summary: Treasuries rose after a report that the US and Iran reached an agreement to extend a truce and work toward an end to a three-month war that’s ignited an oil-driven surge in inflation.

items.title: Bessent Says Trump Won’t Accept a Bad Deal on Iran: Full Briefing
items.summary: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said that President Trump will not make a bad deal with Iran amid reports of a 60-day truce extension waiting the president's approval. items.summary: Federal Reserve Bank of St. Louis President Alberto Musalem says the central bank is missing on the inflation side of its mandate during an interview with Bloomberg's Stephanie Flanders at a central banking conference in Iceland. (Source: Bloomberg)
